title: erophila vulgaris var. glabrescens
intro: Erophila vulgaris var. glabrescens, also known as Erophila glabrescens, is a plant species within the Brassicaceae family, closely related to cress and mustard. It is a hairy-leaved variety of the more common Erophila vuralis. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an expectorant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Erophila glabrescens are often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves to harness its health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.
